skill.name: docx
skill.description: Create, inspect, edit, and review Word DOCX files using OOXML, docx-js, and Python helper workflows for comments, tracked changes, formatting, and text extraction.
skill.summary: Create and review DOCX files with OOXML editing, comments, tracked changes, and conversion workflows.
skill.author: Azeem-2

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many command, filesystem, network, and heuristic warnings. Most XML namespace URLs, schema terms, long template lines, and docx-js examples are false positives, but unsafe archive extraction and privileged dependency installation guidance are confirmed high-risk issues. No evidence found of malicious data exfiltration, credential theft, or prompt-injection text that tells the auditor to skip review.

high_findings.title: Unsafe Office Archive Extraction
high_findings.description: The unpack and validation helpers extract user-supplied Office archives with extractall without validating member paths. A crafted DOCX, PPTX, or XLSX archive can write outside the intended output directory through zip-slip paths.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct use of ZipFile.extractall on user-provided Office files is visible in multiple helpers. The code does not show path normalization or rejection of absolute or parent-directory zip entries before extraction.

high_findings.title: Privileged Dependency Installation Guidance
high_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to install system packages with sudo apt-get as part of normal dependency setup. Marketplace users may not expect a document skill to request privileged package installation, which can change the host system.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The sudo commands are explicit and are presented as dependency setup. This is not malicious by itself, but it is high operational risk in automated agent environments.

medium_findings.title: External Document Tool Execution
medium_findings.description: The workflow runs pandoc, LibreOffice, Poppler, npm, and git commands to convert, validate, or compare documents. Most calls use fixed command names and argument arrays, but they process user-supplied document paths and generated files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are legitimate for document workflows and subprocess calls use argument lists, reducing injection risk. Residual risk remains because untrusted documents are passed to large external parsers and converters.

medium_findings.title: Filesystem Modification of User Documents
medium_findings.description: The skill writes DOCX packages, edits XML files, copies document directories, and deletes failed validation output. These operations are expected for the skill but can overwrite or remove files if users supply unsafe output paths.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem behavior is core functionality, not covert behavior. The risk is moderate because path handling relies on user-provided arguments and normal file permissions.

low_findings.title: Static Network URL Findings Are Mostly Namespace References
low_findings.description: Many hardcoded URL findings are OOXML, XML, and Microsoft namespace identifiers inside schemas or templates. These are not network calls by themselves and do not show data exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations define namespace strings and schema mappings. I did not find evidence that these values are used to send data to external services.

low_findings.title: Schema and Template Heuristics Appear Benign
low_findings.description: Weak-crypto, C2 keyword, path traversal, and long-line heuristics are frequently triggered by OOXML schema names, attributes, relative schema imports, and compact XML templates. These appear to be document-format artifacts rather than malicious logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context is Office schema or example documentation. No evidence found that these strings implement C2, evasion, or credential access behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Archive extractall Without Zip-Slip Guard
dangerous_patterns.description: User-controlled Office archives are extracted with extractall into output or temporary directories without visible validation of each zip entry path.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is direct and repeated across helpers. This is a known unsafe archive handling pattern when archive contents are untrusted.

dangerous_patterns.title: Agent-Initiated Privileged Package Installation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill lists sudo apt-get install commands for normal use, which can prompt an agent to modify system packages outside a document workspace.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command text is explicit and located in dependency instructions. It requires human or sandbox policy control before use.

content.value_statement: Word documents often need precise edits, comments, and tracked changes that are hard to automate safely. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows for DOCX creation, OOXML inspection, conversion, and review.
actual_capabilities: Create new DOCX files with JavaScript docx-js examples and formatting guidance.
Unpack DOCX, PPTX, and XLSX files for raw OOXML inspection and editing.
Add or review Word comments and tracked changes through Python helper workflows.
Convert DOCX files to Markdown, PDF, or page images using external tools.
Validate edited Office XML against included OOXML schema resources.
Preserve document structure, media references, styles, and revision metadata when used carefully.
limitations: Requires external tools such as pandoc, LibreOffice, Poppler, npm, and Python packages.
Unsafe archive extraction helpers require sandboxing before processing untrusted Office files.
Complex DOCX formatting can still break if OOXML edits are not validated.
The skill is not a legal review service and does not verify document meaning.

use_cases.title: Prepare Contract Redlines
use_cases.description: Apply targeted tracked changes and comments while preserving review metadata and Word compatibility.

use_cases.title: Generate Structured Reports
use_cases.description: Create formatted DOCX reports with headings, tables, lists, headers, footers, and consistent styles.

use_cases.title: Inspect Document Internals
use_cases.description: Unpack Office files, inspect OOXML parts, validate relationships, and repair document structure.

prompt_templates.title: Extract Document Text
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner analysis of a Word file
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the DOCX workflow to extract readable text from this Word document. Preserve headings, lists, and tracked changes when possible.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Styled DOCX
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a professional DOCX report with a title, section headings, a summary table, and consistent paragraph styles.

prompt_templates.title: Add Review Comments
prompt_templates.scenario: Comment on specific clauses or sections
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this DOCX and add Word comments on unclear language, missing definitions, and inconsistent terms. Keep the original text unchanged.

prompt_templates.title: Apply Tracked Changes
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply these requested edits as precise tracked changes in the DOCX. Batch related changes, validate the result, and summarize every revision.

output_examples.input: A user provides a policy DOCX and asks for review comments.
output_examples.output: A revised Word document with comments attached to relevant sections, plus a short summary of the review themes.

output_examples.input: A user asks for a formatted project brief as a DOCX.
output_examples.output: A Word document with a title page, headings, bullet lists, a timeline table, and consistent business formatting.

output_examples.input: A user provides requested contract edits and an existing DOCX.
output_examples.output: A redlined DOCX showing insertions and deletions, with a verification note covering the applied changes.

best_practices: Run document conversion and archive extraction only inside an isolated workspace.
Validate every edited DOCX before sharing it with another user or system.
Back up the original document before applying tracked changes or XML edits.
anti_patterns: Do not run sudo installation commands from the skill without explicit human approval.
Do not unpack untrusted Office files outside a sandboxed temporary directory.
Do not make broad XML replacements when a precise tracked change is required.

faq.question: Can this skill create new Word documents?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes docx-js guidance for creating DOCX files with sections, styles, tables, lists, images, and headers.

faq.question: Can it edit existing DOCX files?
faq.answer: Yes. It can unpack Office files and guide OOXML edits through Python helper workflows.

faq.question: Does it support tracked changes?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes redlining workflows and XML patterns for insertions, deletions, comments, and review metadata.

faq.question: Is it safe for confidential documents?
faq.answer: Use it only in a trusted local workspace. The reviewed files show no exfiltration, but external tools and file operations are required.

faq.question: What tools does it need?
faq.answer: Common workflows may need pandoc, LibreOffice, Poppler, npm docx, Python, and defusedxml.

faq.question: Why is the marketplace risk high?
faq.answer: The helper scripts extract Office archives without zip-slip path checks, and the skill includes sudo package installation guidance.
